mapping.a_element: weak or sparse interactions among the variables that limit how far coupling propagates
mapping.b_element: localization length ξ that sets the range over which sites/modes influence each other

mapping.a_element: delocalization of bias across dimensions (each marginal stays small)
mapping.b_element: long-range-to-short-range crossover of the Lyapunov-spectrum scaling

brief_justification: The overlap is only the loose word '(de)localization' plus a generic 'correlation range controls a spreading/crossover' intuition, not shared machinery: paper A is a Wasserstein-2 bias analysis of unadjusted HMC/underdamped-Langevin integrators built on a matrix-polynomial propagator framework, whereas paper B studies the Lyapunov-spectrum scaling transition of nonlinear unitary maps under quasiperiodic vs Anderson disorder. Paper A has no Lyapunov exponents, dynamical map, or physical localization transition, and paper B has no sampler bias, Wasserstein bound, or propagator polynomial, so no role-for-role governing process transfers.
